label: Important entity may have become essential due to size increase
warning: This entity is classified as an important entity but reports 250 or more employees, which meets the large-enterprise threshold under Recommendation 2003/361/EC. Annex I entities with ≥250 employees (or >€50M turnover or >€43M balance sheet) must be classified as essential entities under Article 3(1)(a). Seek legal advice and notify the competent authority if the classification should be updated.

label: Important entity may have become essential due to turnover exceeding €50M
warning: This entity is classified as an important entity but has an annual turnover exceeding €50M, which meets the large-enterprise threshold. Annex I entities exceeding this turnover must be classified as essential entities. Verify current classification with your competent authority.

label: Entity may be designated as essential as sole provider in Member State
warning: This entity has been identified as the sole provider of an essential service in a Member State. Under Article 2(2)(b) and Article 3(1)(f), such entities may be identified as essential entities by the Member State regardless of size. Confirm with the competent authority whether a formal designation has been or should be made.

label: Entity providing digital infrastructure services may also qualify as essential regardless of size
warning: Entities in the digital_infrastructure sector include several sub-types (trust service providers, TLD name registries, DNS service providers, public electronic communications providers) that are classified as essential entities regardless of size under Article 3(1)(b)-(d). If this entity is a trust service provider, TLD registry, DNS service provider, or public electronic communications provider, it should be classified as essential — consult with the competent authority to confirm.
human_review_note: The digital_infrastructure sector is broad and includes both general entities (classified as important if medium-sized) and specific sub-types that are essential regardless of size. This warning is intentionally broad — reviewer should confirm the entity's specific sub-type.

label: Former NIS1 operator of essential services is automatically classified as essential
warning: Entities identified as operators of essential services (OES) under the previous NIS1 Directive (Directive (EU) 2016/1148) are automatically classified as essential entities under Article 3(1)(h), regardless of size. If this entity held OES status before 18 October 2024, it should be reclassified as an essential entity.
